text: Human resources serve as the cornerstone of organizational success, acting as the primary catalyst for achieving strategic objectives. While modern enterprises leverage sophisticated technology and comprehensive data, these assets remain dormant without skilled personnel capable of operationalizing them. Consequently, the value of any physical or informational resource is ultimately predicated on the quality and competence of the workforce. Human Resource Management (HRM) provides the vital framework for planning, organizing, directing, and controlling these human assets to ensure organizational efficiency. This administrative process encompasses key operational functions, including procurement, professional development, compensation, integration, maintenance, and separation. A foundational element of this framework is job analysis. By systematically evaluating the specific duties and requisite competencies associated with each role, job analysis enables organizations to align human capabilities with strategic needs, ensuring the right talent is positioned to drive institutional growth.
